title: Smoky Fragrant Slovakian Slovakian Dish Grill
description: A lazy-Sunday slovakian dish featuring sweet smoked paprika, robata-grill, and a briny flavor profile. Perfect for paleo-friendly meals.
instructions: Preheat the robata-grill to 220 degrees celsius.
Rinse the pork shoulder and pat it dry with a paper towel.
Mix the smoked paprika, garlic, caraway seeds, salt, and black pepper in a small bowl.
Rub the spice mixture all over the pork shoulder, making sure to coat it evenly.
Place the pork shoulder in a large kazan-cauldron and add the apple cider vinegar, onion, potatoes, bay leaves, and water.
Add the lard to the kazan-cauldron and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the kazan-cauldron with a lid and transfer it to the preheated robata-grill.
Cook the pork shoulder for 2 hours, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 90 degrees celsius.
Remove the kazan-cauldron from the robata-grill and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Shred the pork shoulder with two forks and serve it hot.
Serve the pork shoulder with the cooking juices spooned over the top.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley or dill, if desired.
